fall_armyworm.symptoms: Small holes in leaves, sawdust-like frass, caterpillars inside whorl
fall_armyworm.treatment: Apply Bt (Bacillus thuringiensis) spray or neem-based pesticide. For severe cases, use emamectin benzoate 5% SG at 0.4g/litre. Scout fields early morning when larvae are active.
fall_armyworm.prevention: Early planting, intercropping with beans or cowpeas, use push-pull technology with Desmodium and Napier grass. Pheromone traps at 5/acre.
fall_armyworm.organic_option: Spray neem seed kernel extract (NSKE) 5%, or Beauveria bassiana at 2g/litre. Sand + lime in whorl.

late_blight.symptoms: Water-soaked lesions on leaves turning brown/black, white mold on undersides in humid weather, fruit rot
late_blight.treatment: Apply mancozeb 75% WP at 2.5g/litre or copper oxychloride every 7 days. Remove and burn affected leaves. Ensure good air circulation.
late_blight.prevention: Use resistant varieties (Arka Rakshak for tomato), avoid overhead irrigation, stake plants, apply preventive copper spray before rains
late_blight.organic_option: Bordeaux mixture (1%), Trichoderma viride soil drench

bacterial_wilt.symptoms: Rapid wilting of entire plant, brown discoloration of stem vascular tissue, sticky bacterial ooze when stem is cut and dipped in water
bacterial_wilt.treatment: No chemical cure. Remove and burn infected plants immediately. Solarize soil with clear plastic for 6 weeks. Do not replant same crop for 3+ seasons.
bacterial_wilt.prevention: Use certified disease-free seeds, rotate with non-solanaceous crops (maize, onion), improve drainage, use resistant varieties, grafting on resistant rootstock
bacterial_wilt.organic_option: Pseudomonas fluorescens soil drench, Trichoderma + neem cake soil amendment

northern_leaf_blight.symptoms: Long elliptical gray-green to tan lesions on leaves, starting from lower leaves, can kill entire leaf
northern_leaf_blight.treatment: Apply mancozeb or azoxystrobin fungicide at first sign. Remove heavily infected lower leaves.
northern_leaf_blight.prevention: Plant resistant hybrids, rotate crops, plow under crop residue after harvest
northern_leaf_blight.organic_option: Trichoderma spray, proper spacing for air flow

rust.symptoms: Small orange-brown pustules on leaves and stems, powdery spores when touched, leaf drying
rust.treatment: Apply propiconazole 25% EC at 1ml/litre or tebuconazole at first sign. Repeat after 14 days if needed.
rust.prevention: Plant resistant varieties, avoid dense planting, remove volunteer plants, early sowing

powdery_mildew.symptoms: White powdery coating on leaves, stunted growth, leaf curling and drying
powdery_mildew.treatment: Apply sulfur-based fungicide (wettable sulfur 3g/litre) or hexaconazole. Neem oil also effective.
powdery_mildew.prevention: Good spacing (don't crowd plants), avoid overhead watering, plant in full sun, resistant varieties
powdery_mildew.organic_option: Neem oil 2ml/litre, baking soda spray (5g/litre + soap)

aphid_infestation.symptoms: Clusters of small green/black insects on undersides of leaves, sticky honeydew, curling leaves, sooty mold, virus transmission
aphid_infestation.treatment: Spray with neem oil 2ml/litre or imidacloprid 0.3ml/litre for severe cases. Introduce ladybugs (25-50 per plant).
aphid_infestation.prevention: Companion planting with marigolds, garlic, or coriander. Remove weeds. Use reflective mulch. Yellow sticky traps.
aphid_infestation.organic_option: Neem oil, soap spray (5ml liquid soap per litre), garlic-chilli extract

pink_bollworm.symptoms: Rosette flowers (petals stuck together), green bolls with holes, larvae inside bolls eating lint and seeds
pink_bollworm.treatment: Pheromone traps at 5/acre for monitoring. Spray profenophos or cypermethrin if >10% boll damage.
pink_bollworm.prevention: Timely sowing (before June 15), destroy crop residue, use Bt cotton varieties, avoid late-season irrigation
pink_bollworm.organic_option: Mass trapping with pheromone traps (25/acre), neem seed kernel extract, Trichogramma releases

stem_borer.symptoms: Dead hearts in young plants, whiteheads in older plants, bore holes in stems, frass visible
stem_borer.treatment: Apply carbofuran granules in leaf whorl, or spray chlorantraniliprole. Remove dead hearts.
stem_borer.prevention: Early planting, avoid excess nitrogen, light traps (1/acre), remove stubble after harvest
stem_borer.organic_option: Trichogramma egg parasitoid release (8 cards/acre), neem cake in field

downy_mildew.symptoms: Yellow patches on upper leaf surface, gray-purple fuzzy growth underneath, stunted growth
downy_mildew.treatment: Metalaxyl + mancozeb (Ridomil Gold) at 2g/litre. Apply before disease spreads.
downy_mildew.prevention: Seed treatment with metalaxyl, proper drainage, avoid waterlogging, resistant varieties
downy_mildew.organic_option: Bordeaux mixture 0.5%, good drainage and spacing

wilt_fusarium.symptoms: Yellowing of lower leaves on one side first, brown vascular bundles when stem is cut, gradual wilting and death
wilt_fusarium.treatment: No chemical cure once infected. Remove plants. Soil treatment with Trichoderma + neem cake before next planting.
wilt_fusarium.prevention: Resistant varieties, soil solarization, crop rotation with cereals (3 years), seed treatment with carbendazim
wilt_fusarium.organic_option: Trichoderma viride 4g/kg seed treatment, Pseudomonas fluorescens soil drench

whitefly.symptoms: Tiny white flying insects on leaf undersides, sticky honeydew, sooty mold, leaf curl virus transmission
whitefly.treatment: Yellow sticky traps for monitoring. Spray neem oil or spiromesifen for heavy infestation.
whitefly.prevention: Remove weeds (alternate hosts), yellow sticky traps (12/acre), avoid planting near infected fields
whitefly.organic_option: Neem oil 3ml/litre, sticky traps, Encarsia formosa parasitoid

red_rot.symptoms: Red discoloration inside sugarcane stalk, white patches in red area, stale alcohol smell, drying of top leaves
red_rot.treatment: No cure. Remove infected stools. Treat seed setts with carbendazim before planting.
red_rot.prevention: Use disease-free seed material, resistant varieties (Co-86032), hot water treatment of setts at 50°C for 2 hours
red_rot.organic_option: Trichoderma treatment of setts, avoid waterlogging

clay.description: Heavy soil, holds water, cracks when dry. Rich in minerals.
clay.improve: Add organic matter (compost, FYM) 2-3 tonnes/acre. Add gypsum 500kg/acre to break up. Avoid working when wet.
clay.fertilizer_tip: Nutrients release slowly — apply less but longer-lasting forms. Split nitrogen doses.

loam.description: Ideal balanced soil. Good water retention and drainage.
loam.improve: Maintain with regular compost 1-2 tonnes/acre per season. Mulch to retain moisture.
loam.fertilizer_tip: Responds well to balanced NPK. Add micronutrients (zinc, boron) as needed.

sandy.description: Light soil, drains fast, low nutrients. Warms quickly.
sandy.improve: Add compost and manure heavily — 3-4 tonnes/acre. Use drip irrigation. Mulch thickly to retain moisture.
sandy.fertilizer_tip: Nutrients leach fast — apply in small, frequent doses. Slow-release fertilizers preferred.

laterite.description: Red/orange soil, acidic, low in nutrients, common in tropical areas.
laterite.improve: Add lime 200-400kg/acre to correct pH. Heavy compost + FYM. Green manuring with dhaincha/sunhemp.
laterite.fertilizer_tip: Very low phosphorus — apply single super phosphate generously. Add zinc and boron.

black_cotton.description: Very fertile, deep black. Swells when wet, cracks when dry. Called 'regur' soil.
black_cotton.improve: Add mulch to prevent cracking. Raised beds for drainage. Add FYM 2 tonnes/acre. Don't over-irrigate.
black_cotton.fertilizer_tip: Rich in calcium and magnesium. Often low in nitrogen and phosphorus. Add zinc sulfate.

sandy_loam.description: Light textured but with some clay content. Good drainage with moderate retention.
sandy_loam.improve: Regular organic matter addition. Mulching essential in summer.
sandy_loam.fertilizer_tip: Split nitrogen doses. Good response to foliar feeding.

general_tips.composting: Mix green materials (fresh leaves, kitchen scraps) with brown materials (dry leaves, straw) in 1:3 ratio. Turn every 2 weeks. Ready in 2-3 months. Add cow dung to speed up.
general_tips.water_management: Water early morning or late evening to reduce evaporation. Drip irrigation saves 50% water compared to flood irrigation. Mulching reduces water need by 30%.
general_tips.pest_scouting: Check fields twice weekly. Look under leaves and inside plant whorls. Early detection saves 80% of treatment cost. Use yellow/blue sticky traps.
general_tips.seed_selection: Use certified seeds from registered suppliers. Save seeds only from healthy, high-yielding plants. Treat seeds with Trichoderma + carbendazim before planting.
general_tips.market_timing: Prices peak when supply is low — typically just before harvest season. Consider staggered planting for continuous supply and better prices.
general_tips.organic_farming: Use FYM (farmyard manure) 5-8 tonnes/acre as base. Vermicompost 2 tonnes/acre. Panchagavya spray every 15 days. Jeevamrit for soil health.
general_tips.intercropping: Maize + beans is a classic combo (beans fix nitrogen for maize). Cotton + groundnut works in Gujarat. Sugarcane + onion in Maharashtra.
general_tips.crop_rotation: Never plant same family back-to-back. Rotate: cereal → legume → vegetable. This breaks pest/disease cycles and maintains soil fertility.
general_tips.soil_testing: Test soil every 2 years. Costs ₹50-200 at government labs. Tells you exact NPK levels and micronutrient deficiencies. Saves money on unnecessary fertilizer.

india.PM-KISAN: ₹6000/year direct to farmer bank account. Register at pmkisan.gov.in
india.Soil_Health_Card: Free soil testing. Apply at nearest agriculture office.
india.PMFBY: Crop insurance at 1.5-5% premium. Covers flood, drought, pest damage.
india.KCC: Kisan Credit Card — low interest loans up to ₹3 lakh at 4% for farming.
india.eNAM: Sell crops online across mandis. Register at enam.gov.in

kenya.NAAIAP: National Accelerated Agricultural Input Access Programme — subsidized fertilizer
kenya.NCPB: National Cereals and Produce Board — guaranteed minimum prices for maize, wheat

bangladesh.Krishi_Card: Agricultural input subsidy card for fertilizer, seeds

general_tips.storage_tips: Dry grains to 12-13% moisture before storage. Use hermetic bags (PICS, GrainPro) to prevent weevils without chemicals. Store in cool, dry, elevated place away from walls.
general_tips.harvest_timing: Harvest grains at physiological maturity (black layer for maize). Harvest vegetables early morning for better shelf life. Don't harvest wet crops — increases mold risk.

fertilizer_guide.NPK_basics: N (nitrogen) = leaf growth. P (phosphorus) = root/flower. K (potassium) = fruit/disease resistance.
fertilizer_guide.DAP: 18-46-0. Good at planting for root establishment. 50kg/acre typical.
fertilizer_guide.Urea: 46-0-0. Cheapest nitrogen source. Apply in splits, not all at once. 25-50kg/acre per dose.
fertilizer_guide.micronutrients: Zinc sulfate (ZnSO4) 10kg/acre for paddy/maize. Borax 5kg/acre for oilseeds.
